tagline: Vietnamese / Little Saigon parade units for Greater Sacramento — in sequence

org.name: Community Partners Advocate of Little Saigon Sacramento

pitch.headline: March together. Meet your peers before the curb.
pitch.body: Little Saigon Krewe is a proposed way for Vietnamese American and Vietnamese-led partners across Greater Sacramento to show up at civic parades as multiple units in sequence — not one shared banner. The public story is the march. The relationship product is the 9:00–11:00 staging window: peer tribes, greeters, and named Tribe Champions so the krewe lead isn’t everyone’s only contact.

tribes.name: High school
tribes.peers: VSAs and culture clubs — interclub bonding with a light cross-chapter mix
tribes.championProfile: Someone vested in the high school pipeline (e.g. a college VSA outreach lead)

tribes.name: College
tribes.peers: Campus VSAs and VN CARES chapters
tribes.championProfile: A college VSA or VN CARES officer who already convenes peers

tribes.name: Performance
tribes.peers: Lion, dance, and troupe warm-up · spacing · music cues
tribes.championProfile: An active dancer or lion lead who needs coordination anyway

tribes.name: Business
tribes.peers: Chambers, merchants, float and vehicle logistics
tribes.championProfile: A merchant or chamber face who wants peers to meet each other

tribes.name: Family & heritage
tribes.peers: Language school, scouts, pageant, veterans, elders
tribes.championProfile: Someone already in the language / scout / pageant / elder orbit

tribes.name: Service
tribes.championProfile: A peer nonprofit lead (when two or more service orgs show)

championAsk.headline: Become a Tribe Champion
championAsk.body: Champions are stage managers, not stars. Hold one tribe sign, open icebreaker prompts, split crowded tribes into small circles, and give that lane a named face — weeks before parade day. Vested interest beats an available body.

wishlist.headline: Krewe wishlist
wishlist.body: In-kind needs for Golden Harvest — like a gift registry. No dollar amounts yet. Email to pledge what you can cover.

items.detail: Hospitality during the staging window (~9:00–11:00) before the march. Packaged meals, snacks, or gift vouchers all work.

transit.headline: Getting there from Elk Grove
transit.intro: Capitol Mall is downtown Sacramento. Many high school members don’t drive — public transit is a real option for Saturday staging (~9:00 check-in).
steps: Take a SacRT Elk Grove local bus toward Cosumnes River College (CRC) — the southern end of the Blue Line.
Board Blue Line light rail toward Watt / I-80 (downtown direction).
Exit at 8th & Capitol, 9th & K, or Archives Plaza (11th & O) — short walk to Capitol Mall staging.
Return: board Blue Line toward Cosumnes River College (check the headsign), then Elk Grove bus home.
notes: Saturday schedules differ from weekday rush-hour — Elk Grove weekday-only commuter buses (e.g. E13) won’t help parade morning. Use local EG routes + Blue Line, and verify times the week before.
Light rail uses one-way streets downtown; your outbound stop and return stop may differ.
Florin / south Sacramento riders can often skip the Elk Grove bus leg and board Blue Line closer to home.
transit.agencyNote: We’re exploring which agency to approach for youth or group transit support on parade day. This page is the practical how-to meanwhile — not a promise of free rides yet.

links.label: Light rail to downtown (Blue Line / Elk Grove)
